prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Warhammer 40,000 Dark Imperium inspired grimdark science-fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: dual_continent. Draw two large fictional planetary continents as an Imperial war-zone atlas in gothic sci-fi cartography with mechanicus technical overlay and toxic ochre atmosphere, separated by a dramatic non-vertical ocean passage: diagonal and S-shaped straits, polluted gulfs, broken continental shelves, minefield shoals, wreck channels, and jagged narrows rather than a simple straight split. One continent contains hive city mega-structures, cathedral-fortress spires, underhive slums, manufactorum belts, void-port platforms, and Imperial defense lines; the other contains industrial wastelands, toxic promethium refineries, ruined zones, xenos-contaminated badlands, ash deserts, Chaos-tainted war fronts, frozen polar fortresses, and irradiated marshes. Complex realistic coastlines on both continents: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves. Islands are unevenly scattered between and around the continents with varied size and shape, including refinery isles, fortress-monastery islands, quarantine rocks, void-shield harbors, and wreck-strewn reefs. Large detailed grimdark planetary atlas, gothic compass and tactical grid allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
